reasoning: Scoring 82 or more points in an NBA game is an exceptionally rare event, having occurred only once in the history of the league (Wilt Chamberlain's 100 points in 1962). Kobe Bryant scored exactly 81 points in 2006. The highest scores in recent seasons have been Luka Doncic's 73 points and Joel Embiid's 70 points in the 2023-24 season, and Donovan Mitchell and Damian Lillard's 71 points in 2022-23. In the 2024-25 season, the highest reported single-game scores are Jokic's 61 points, Fox's 60 points, and Giannis's 59 points. Furthermore, the 2024-25 NBA regular season typically finishes by mid-April (today is the last day of the regular season), and historic trends show playoff scoring is more difficult and lower (playoff record is just 63 points by Jordan). With essentially no regular season games left and zero indication that any player approached this extraordinary total this year, the probability remains at the absolute floor.
